data.title: Microsoft in focus as Wedbush says tariffs causing 'real damage,' capex uncertainty
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) was in focus on Thursday as Wedbush Securities said the recent uncertainty around tariffs could drive further capex uncertainty.

Shares fell 1.4% in premarket trading.

The back-and-forth between implementing tariffs, then pausing them for 90 days, and the rise in U.S. bond yields has created “real damage to the corporate spending mentality,” Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives said.

“We still maintain our Outperform [rating] and remain long term bullish on MSFT but unfortunately the tariff 'game of poker' is adding a high level of near-term uncertainty to any company with supply chains/cost inputs around China and those tariffs remain very much on the table,” Ives wrote in a note to clients. As such, he cut his estimates for the June quarter and all of fiscal 2026 and lowered his price target to $475 from $550.

Delving a bit deeper, the implementation of tariffs could result in anywhere between 10% to 15% of cloud and artificial intelligence initiatives being pushed out or slowed down as a result of the uncertainty, Ives explained. And Microsoft, with its Azure cloud unit, is likely to be “front and center” in the uncertainty. 

However, investors could be forgiving and “look past” the June quarter and view it as a “mulligan” due to the uncertainty, if a number of deals and projects are pushed out to the September and December quarters.

“We are heading into a very bumpy period as we expect a tech earning season that will be full of uncertainty, lack of guidance from many tech/China exposed tech players, and investors will need to buckle the seat belt and look past the next few quarters of choppiness and hold the tech winners through the storm....including MSFT,” Ives added.

Microsoft is set to report its next quarterly results on April 30. A consensus of analysts expect the Satya Nadella-led firm to earn $3.23 per share on $68.51B in revenue.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Investor sentiment continues to stay bearish - AAII
data.description:
Bearish sentiment among individual investors continued to stay high as uncertainty about the ongoing trade dispute following President Donal Trump’s constantly changing tariff policy dominates headlines.

The American Association of Individual Investors said in its survey for the week ended April 9 that the bearish camp (those who think the market will be lower in the next six months) fell to 58.9% after surging to a one-year high of 61.9% the week before.

Bulls rose to 28.5% this week from 21.8%, the week earlier.

Wall Street’s volatility had become the norm over this week before Trump's announcement that he would issue a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for more than 75 countries who had reached out to the U.S. for negotiations, leading to stock indexes skyrocketing on Wednesday.

However, various analysts are still warning that on recession risks due to Trump’s tariff actions. The March core CPI, which excludes the volatile food and energy categories, is also expected to rise 0.3% in March, compared with the 0.2% increase in February, according to the consensus of economists.

According to AAII, neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, showed a decline to 12.5% for the week, versus 16.3% last week. The weekly survey, which has been conducted by the AAII since 1987, asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

The S&P500 (SP500) gained over 1% in the last one week, while the Dow (DJI) and Nasdaq (COMP:IND) advanced 0.15% and over 3%, respectively, mostly helped by Wednesday’s gain.

data.title: Shipper Maersk, bellwether for global trade, jumps 10% on Trump’s tariff pause
data.description:
Danish shipping giant A.P. Moeller-Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) +9.2% in European trading on Thursday after surging more than 10% earlier, a day after President Trump announced a 90-day delay in applying most tariffs against its trading partners, although the reprieve does not apply to China, which Trump slapped with a 125% levy after it imposed retaliatory measures.

Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) shares had tumbled 25% in the past month, mostly due to concerns over global trade as the U.S. threatened to unleash a worldwide trade war.

Germany's Hapag-Lloyd (OTCPK:HLAGF) (OTCPK:HPGLY), another major name in container shipping, traded +7.5%.

Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) warned last week that the tariffs will hurt the global economy and delay goods currently in transit.

"The news of Trump backing down from tariffs is a big positive for markets, with many saying that the worst case scenario is now off the table," Morningstar chief equity strategist Michael Field wrote, but the overhang of a global trade war is "likely to persist for some time," and investors will remain wary of increasing tensions with steep tariffs remaining for China.

data.title: Baylin Technologies secures C$1.7M purchase order
Verastem initiated Buy at Jefferies on a potential FDA nod for ovarian cancer therapy
data.description:
Jefferies on Thursday launched its coverage of oncology-focused biotech Verastem (NASDAQ:VSTM) with a Buy rating and $15 per share target, citing a potential FDA nod for its ovarian cancer drug combo avutometinib + defactinib over the coming weeks.

In January, the FDA accepted VSTM’s new drug application for the treatment combo targeted at adults with recurrent KRAS mutant low-grade serous ovarian cancer (LGSOC) under the agency’s priority review, granting June 30 as the target action date.

Expecting the FDA to clear the first drug for KRAS mutant LGSOC, a rare cancer type, by June 30, analyst Kelly Shi wrote, “Our KOL feedback suggests strong adoption enthusiasm upon approval, and we believe VSTM is well-positioned to gain a foothold in this untapped ovarian cancer market.”

Jefferies projects a total addressable market worth $1.7B for KRAS mutant LGSOC, where the avutometinib + defactinib combo is expected to generate $282M, $128M, and $46M in peak risk-adjusted sales in the U.S., EU, and Japan, respectively, with a 70% probability of success.

Additionally, Shi expects Verastem (NASDAQ:VSTM) to create “substantial value” subject to positive initial data from its oral KRAS G12D dual ON/OFF inhibitor, VS-7375. Developed in partnership with Chinese biotech GenFleet Therapeutics, VS-7375 is set to generate data from a China-based Phase 1 trial in mid-2025.

data.title: Atomic Minerals announces option agreement to acquire Saskatchewan mineral claims
data.description:
• Atomic Minerals (TSXV:ATOM:CA) announced on Thursday that it has entered into an option agreement amongst the legal and beneficial holder of two mineral claims totaling approximately 5,355 hectares located in the province of Saskatchewan.
  • Pursuant to the option agreement, the vendor has granted the company the exclusive right to acquire a 100% interest in the property.
  • To exercise the option and acquire a 100% interest in the property, the company must issue 5.5 million common shares to the vendor and pay $50,000 in cash to the vendor within three years from the effective date of the option agreement.
  • The company also has the right to purchase a 3% net smelter return royalty held by an arm's-length third party by issuing 5.5 million consideration shares to the royalty holder.
  • The Mozzie Lake project consists of two properties situated in the Pinkham Lake Zone approximately 25km northeast of the Athabasca Basin, located within the Charlebois-Higgingson Lake Uranium District.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lazard expects lower AUM as of March end
data.description:
Financial advisory and asset management firm Lazard (NYSE:LAZ) announced on Thursday preliminary AUM of ~$227.42B, down from $232.88B at the end of the prior month.

Shares were trading +4.99% pre-market at $40.36.

March AUM takes into account a market depreciation of $5.4B, net outflows of $3.1B, and foreign exchange appreciation of $3.1B.

Sector-wise, Equity AUM is expected at $174.13B, Fixed Income at $44.02B, and Other at $9.27B.

data.title: Main Street Capital originates $130M new or increased commitments in Q1
data.description:
Main Street Capital (NYSE:MAIN) originated new or increased commitments in its private loan portfolio of $130.4M and funded total investments across its private loan portfolio with a cost basis totaling $138.2M, the company said on Thursday. That's an increase from the $123.4M in new or increased commitments in Q4 2024.

As of March 31, 2025, the company's private loan portfolio included total investments at a cost of ~$2.0B across 90 different companies. That's roughly the same total cost as of Dec. 31, 2024, which was across 91 unique borrowers.

The private loan portfolio, as a percentage of cost, included 94.7% invested in first lien debt investments and 5.3% invested in equity investments or other securities.

Among the larger commitments in Q1 2025 were:

  • $55.4M in first lien senior secured loan, $9.6M in a first lien senior revolver, and $1.5M in equity in a designer and manufacturer of engineered electrical power distribution solutions to datacenters and other power applications;
  • Increased commitment of $9.6M in incremental first lien senior secured loan, $2.4M in an incremental first lien senior secured revolver, and $0.4M in incremental equity to a provider of highly technical specialty services to datacenters and various other diverse end markets.

Main Street Capital (NYSE:MAIN) stock slipped 0.5% in Thursday premarket trading.

data.title: CarMax GAAP EPS of $0.58 misses by $0.08, revenue of $6B beats by $30M
data.description:
• CarMax press release (NYSE:KMX): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.58 misses by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $6B beats by $30M.
  • Retail used unit sales increased 6.2% and comparable store used unit sales increased 5.1%; wholesale units increased 3.1%.
  • Total gross profit of $667.9 million increased 13.9%, driven by unit volumes and strong unit margin performance.
  • Repurchased $98.5 million in shares of common stock in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2025.
  •

data.title: Mobilicom climbs 6%, secures scale orders from Asia
data.description:
Mobilicom (NASDAQ:MOB) received initial production-scale orders from a Tier-1 Asian conglomerate and manufacturer of robotics platforms that are sold to customers in the military and industrial markets.

The orders consisted of products from three of Mobilicom’s product families, including Mobilicom’s MCU Mesh Networking, SkyHopper Datalinks, and Mobile Ground Stations.

“While our strength to date has been sales in the U.S., Europe, and Israel, we are now experiencing increased commercial traction in Asia. These recent orders by a Tier-1 robotics manufacturer also demonstrate new momentum for Mobilicom in robotics, expanding beyond our well-established track record with drones.” said CEO and Founder Oren Elkayam. 

The stock price jumped 6.3% on Thursday during pre-market hours of trading.
